title: viola helena
intro: Viola helena, also known as common winter violet or dog's violet, is a flowering plant species in the viola genus. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Viola helena include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe digestive problems, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds, coughs, and flu. Viola helena can be prepared through herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be made by steeping the plant's flowers, leaves, or roots in hot water or a solvent.
